item.title: Creating the smallest shockwave!
item.description: In this video I show you what happens when you smash two giant steel ball bearings together! You get an amazing shock-wave that can burn paper and send it flying it sonic speeds!

media:content.media:title: Creating the smallest shockwave!
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you what happens when you smash two giant steel ball bearings together! You get an amazing shock-wave that can burn paper and send it flying it sonic speeds!

item.title: Controlling a plant with my brain
item.description: I show you how to make plants communicate with each other through action potentials. I even connect it to my arm and control the plant as well.

media:content.media:title: Controlling a plant with my brain
media:content.media:description: I show you how to make plants communicate with each other through action potentials. I even connect it to my arm and control the plant as well.

item.title: Don't add ashes to your sugar

media:content.media:title: Don't add ashes to your sugar

item.title: When things fall up
item.description: In this video I show you some objects the roll uphill instead of down. Then I talk about how it is possible and how it is still falling down.

media:content.media:title: When things fall up
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you some objects the roll uphill instead of down. Then I talk about how it is possible and how it is still falling down.

item.title: Can high voltage power lines light up a bulb in your hand?
item.description: Discover why a light bulb can glow under high-voltage power lines. But does this strange effect mean power lines are actually dangerous?

media:content.media:title: Can high voltage power lines light up a bulb in your hand?
media:content.media:description: Discover why a light bulb can glow under high-voltage power lines. But does this strange effect mean power lines are actually dangerous?

item.title: The "ball bouncing on a ruler trick" explained
item.description: I talk about the viral ball bouncing on a ruler trick and why it works

media:content.media:title: The "ball bouncing on a ruler trick" explained
media:content.media:description: I talk about the viral ball bouncing on a ruler trick and why it works

item.title: Is it safe to eat pure DNA?
item.description: In this video I show you how to extract DNA! Then I eat it just to see what it tastes like! I take the DNA out of strawberries, kiwis and even shrimp! This is an amazing experiment that you could do in your own home with simple ingredients. I talk about why the reaction happens and then finally eat all of the DNA at the end!

media:content.media:title: Is it safe to eat pure DNA?
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you how to extract DNA! Then I eat it just to see what it tastes like! I take the DNA out of strawberries, kiwis and even shrimp! This is an amazing experiment that you could do in your own home with simple ingredients. I talk about why the reaction happens and then finally eat all of the DNA at the end!

item.title: Are black bubbles possible?
item.description: In this video I make the word's first black bubble solution! I use a special kind of bubbles and coal black food coloring. I talk about the physics of light penetration and how you could even make transparent metal! And then finally I put the touchable bubbles in the vacuum chamber to see what happens!

media:content.media:title: Are black bubbles possible?
media:content.media:description: In this video I make the word's first black bubble solution! I use a special kind of bubbles and coal black food coloring. I talk about the physics of light penetration and how you could even make transparent metal! And then finally I put the touchable bubbles in the vacuum chamber to see what happens!

item.title: Why AC electricity doesn’t behave the way you think
item.description: In this video I talk about why AC currents usually hide a DC bias

media:content.media:title: Why AC electricity doesn’t behave the way you think
media:content.media:description: In this video I talk about why AC currents usually hide a DC bias

item.title: Steel gets heavier when you burn it
item.description: I show you how mass is always conserved, except when it isn't...
